era: Hip-Hop/Experimental. E=6 -- noise-experimental partnership, Butler + Blood. RETURN from degree 106. Back-to-back Butler (170-171). Eleventh consecutive E=6 (161-171). v1.49.311.

location.formative: Butler (Central District) + Blood (Seattle independent scene). Noise-experimental hip-hop. Sub Pop.
location.career: Knife Knights. Ishmael Butler + Erik Blood. 2017-present. Sub Pop.

members.name: Erik Blood
members.note: Seattle. Multi-instrumentalist. Solo career. Dark experimental production.

associated_musicians.name: Tendai Maraire
associated_musicians.note: Not in Knife Knights but part of Butler network.

theory_nodes.concept: Back-to-back Butler (170-171) -- solo then partnership at consecutive degrees. Individual vs duo. Unconstrained freedom vs collaborative chemistry. The densest artist cluster in the engine.
theory_nodes.description: Back-to-back Butler. Solo then duo. The densest cluster.

theory_nodes.concept: Noise-experimental hip-hop as E=6 -- the most extreme extension of hip-hop's experimental frontier. Anti-commercial. Confrontational. Noise, industrial, and chaos.
examples: Vocal buried in noise or shouted over chaos.

genealogy.relationship: Knife Knights at 171. Most extreme Butler extension.

theory_nodes.concept: 65-degree return gap -- Knife Knights at 106, returns at 171. Network accumulation across 65 degrees provides new context for the return.
examples: 171: return with full Butler arc documented.
theory_nodes.description: 65-degree return gap. Context accumulation recontextualizes the return.

genealogy.relationship: Knife Knights returns at 171. Richer context.

cross_references.relationship: BACK-TO-BACK BUTLER! Solo (170) then partnership (171). Individual vs duo. Consecutive Butler entries.
RETURN! First at 106. Returns at 171. 65-degree gap. Full Butler arc now documented between appearances.
BUTLER NETWORK! Shabazz return (157) and Knife Knights return (171). Two Butler partnerships returning. Same Sub Pop infrastructure.
BUTLER ARC ORIGIN! First Butler entry (103). Knife Knights return at 171. 68-degree span. Six total entries.
